content.content:
# Concrete Technical Claims About CSS Masonry Layout

## Browser Support Status

**Limited availability - Experimental feature:**
- "This feature is not Baseline because it does not work in some of the most widely-used browsers."
- "This is an experimental technology"
- "Check the Browser compatibility table carefully before using this in production."

## Syntax and Property Names

**Display values:**
- `display: grid-lanes` (most common use case)
- `display: inline-grid-lanes`

**Required property:**
- Must be used with [`grid-template-columns`](/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/grid-template-columns) or [`grid-template-rows`](/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/grid-template-rows)

**Example:**
```css
.grid {
  display: grid-lanes;
  gap: 10px;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(120px, 1fr));
}
```

## How Masonry Differs from Regular Grid

**Core difference:**
"Masonry layout is a layout method where one axis uses a typical strict grid layout, most often columns, and the other a **stacking** (masonry) layout."

"On the stacking axis, rather than sticking to a strict grid with gaps being left after shorter items, the items in the following row rise up to fill the gaps."

## Grid Axis Controls

**Spanning items work as expected:**
- Items can span multiple tracks using the `span` keyword
- Items with definite placement are positioned before masonry layout occurs

**Example with spanning:**
```css
.span-2 {
  grid-column-end: span 2;
}
```

**Line-based positioning supported:**
```css
.positioned {
  grid-column: 2 / 4;
}
```

## Fallback Behavior

"In browsers that do not support masonry, regular grid auto-placement will be used instead."

## Related Properties

- [`grid-auto-flow`](/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties/grid-auto-flow) - for controlling grid auto-placement

## Specification Reference

"Level 3 of the CSS grid layout specification defines masonry layout (also known as **grid-lanes** layout)"

Source: [CSS Grid Layout Module Level 3 - grid-lanes-model](https://drafts.csswg.org/css-grid-3/#grid-lanes-model)
